description: Distribute separate things over an area or send them in several directions. Seed, loose material, light, or a group leaving one place can be scattered. 'thophe' (sow) is planting; 'welura' (spread) can extend one material or pattern, while 'thiwera' keeps multiple items or paths in view.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the breath pass into high-front 'i'. Round the lips and lift the back of the tongue for 'w', then open into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Tap the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and release into open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The stressed middle syllable pulls away from the narrow dental opening, and the final 'ra' opens farther. That audible widening suits movement into several directions. Its resemblance to 'thinoe' (seed) is a felt association, not a derivation.
usage_notes: The scattered thing is the object in a transitive clause; a group can also scatter intransitively. The Babel transmutation deliberately activates the sowing image by comparing people to seeds. Ordinary 'thiwera' does not promise that dispersal is welcome or that anything will grow.

semantic_domains.activity: deliberate distribution of separate things over an area
semantic_domains.nature: seed, light, or organisms dispersed through a living place
semantic_domains.spatial: separate items or paths moving out from one place
